title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots, garnished with green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of authentic Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with tangy, spicy, and savory flavors.

description: Hot and Sour Soup is a quintessential Chinese comfort food, known for its invigorating blend of spicy heat and tangy zest. This classic soup features a rich, savory broth brimming with tender tofu, earthy mushrooms, and crisp bamboo shoots. It's a delightful culinary experience that awakens the palate with every spoonful.

ingredients.item: Lean pork loin or chicken breast

ingredients.notes: or to taste, essential for authentic flavor
mixed with 1/4 cup cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried shiitake mushrooms and black fungus in warm water for 20-30 minutes until soft. Remove stems from shiitake, then julienne both types of mushrooms. Press firm tofu to remove excess water, then julienne along with bamboo shoots. If using pork or chicken, julienne the meat.

instructions.title: Sauté Meat (Optional)
instructions.text: If adding pork or chicken, heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a large pot or wok over medium-high heat. Add the julienned meat and stir-fry until it is cooked through and lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes. Remove the cooked meat and set it aside.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Vegetables
instructions.text: Pour the chicken or vegetable broth into the same pot. Add the rehydrated julienned mushrooms and bamboo shoots. Bring the broth to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Let it cook for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld beautifully.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the rice vinegar, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, white pepper, chili oil, and salt into the simmering broth. Mix thoroughly to combine all the flavors. Taste the soup and adjust the seasonings as needed, adding more vinegar for tang or white pepper for extra heat.

instructions.title: Thicken and Add Tofu
instructions.text: Add the julienned tofu and the cooked meat (if you used it) to the soup. Give it a gentle stir. Slowly pour the cornstarch slurry into the simmering soup while continuously stirring. Continue stirring until the soup thickens to your desired consistency, which usually takes about 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Egg and Finish
instructions.text: While stirring the soup in a circular motion, slowly drizzle in the beaten egg in a thin, steady stream. The egg will cook instantly, forming delicate ribbons throughout the soup. Remove the pot from the heat, stir in the sesame oil, and garnish generously with chopped green onions before serving immediately.

tips: For an authentic taste, do not skimp on white pepper; it provides a unique aromatic heat distinct from chili, which is crucial for Hot and Sour Soup.
To achieve perfect egg ribbons, ensure the soup is simmering gently and stir continuously in one direction while slowly drizzling in the beaten egg.
Hot and Sour Soup is best served immediately. If reheating leftovers, you might need to add a splash of broth and re-thicken slightly with a fresh cornstarch slurry.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Chinese cuisine with one of its most beloved and iconic dishes: Hot and Sour Soup. This fiery yet tangy concoction is a staple on menus worldwide, celebrated for its complex flavor profile that tantalizes the taste buds with every spoonful. More than just a starter, Hot and Sour Soup offers a symphony of textures and aromas, making it a truly satisfying experience. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore Asian flavors, mastering this soup is a rewarding endeavor. If you're looking for an authentic Hot and Sour Soup recipe that delivers on taste and tradition, you've come to the right place. We'll guide you through its origins, essential ingredients, and the simple steps to recreate this restaurant-quality dish in your own kitchen.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

Hot and Sour Soup, or "Suan La Tang" in Mandarin, is a quintessential Chinese soup renowned for its distinctive balance of spicy and sour notes. Originating from Sichuan province, though variations exist across different regions of China, its popularity has spread globally. The "hot" typically comes from white pepper and sometimes chili oil, while the "sour" is derived from Chinese black vinegar. Beyond these foundational flavors, the soup is characterized by a rich, savory broth, often thickened with cornstarch, and a delightful mix of ingredients. Common additions include shredded pork, tofu, various mushrooms (such as shiitake and wood ear), bamboo shoots, and often a whisked egg ribboned through at the end. It's a comforting, warming, and incredibly flavorful dish that stimulates the appetite and soothes the soul.

## Ingredients You Need for Hot and Sour Soup

Creating an authentic Hot and Sour Soup recipe requires a blend of fresh produce and essential pantry staples. Here’s what you’ll typically need:

*   **For the Broth:**
    *   6-8 cups chicken or vegetable broth (low sodium)
    *   2-3 dried shiitake mushrooms (for soaking and adding umami)
*   **Proteins:**
    *   4-6 ounces lean pork loin, shredded (or chicken breast, shrimp, or firm tofu for vegetarian)
    *   4-6 ounces firm or extra-firm tofu, pressed and cut into small strips
*   **Vegetables & Aromatics:**
    *   1/2 cup bamboo shoots, shredded
    *   1/2 cup wood ear mushrooms, rehydrated and shredded (or fresh shiitake, sliced)
    *   2-3 cloves garlic, minced
    *   1 tablespoon fresh ginger, minced
    *   2 green onions, sliced (white and green parts separated)
*   **Seasonings & Flavorings:**
    *   1/4 cup Chinese black vinegar (Chinkiang vinegar is preferred)
    *   2-3 tablespoons soy sauce (light or regular)
    *   1-2 tablespoons sesame oil
    *   1-2 teaspoons white pepper (essential for the "hot" element)
    *   1/2 teaspoon sugar (to balance flavors)
    *   Pinch of red pepper flakes or chili oil (optional, for extra heat)
*   **Thickener & Finisher:**
    *   3-4 tablespoons cornstarch, mixed with 1/4 cup cold water (slurry)
    *   1 large egg, whisked
*   **Garnish:**
    *   Reserved green onion greens, chopped fresh cilantro

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Hot and Sour Soup

Follow this Hot and Sour Soup recipe to create a delicious and authentic bowl from scratch:

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** If using dried shiitake mushrooms, rehydrate them in warm water for at least 30 minutes. Once soft, remove stems, slice thinly, and reserve the soaking liquid. Shred pork, slice tofu, bamboo shoots, and rehydrated wood ear mushrooms. Mince garlic and ginger. Slice green onions, separating whites from greens. Whisk the egg in a small bowl and prepare the cornstarch slurry.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** Heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the green onions. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
3.  **Add Pork and Broth:** Add the shredded pork (or other protein) and stir-fry until it’s no longer pink, about 2-3 minutes. Pour in the chicken or vegetable broth and the reserved shiitake mushroom soaking liquid (strained to remove any grit). Bring to a simmer.
4.  **Add Vegetables and Seasonings:** Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Stir in the soy sauce, Chinese black vinegar, white pepper, and sugar. If desired, add a pinch of red pepper flakes for extra heat. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir to recombine, then slowly pour it into the simmering soup in a thin stream, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. Add more slurry if needed.
6.  **Add Egg and Finish:** Reduce the heat to low. Slowly drizzle the whisked egg into the soup in a thin stream while gently stirring in one direction. This will create beautiful egg ribbons. Turn off the heat.
7.  **Final Touches:** Stir in the sesame oil. Taste and adjust seasonings – you might want more vinegar for sourness, more white pepper for heat, or a touch more soy sauce for saltiness.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Garnish with the green parts of the green onions and fresh cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup

To elevate your Hot and Sour Soup recipe from good to extraordinary, consider these expert tips:

*   **Quality Ingredients:** The flavor of your soup heavily relies on the quality of your broth and vinegar. Use a good quality chicken broth and authentic Chinkiang black vinegar for the most robust taste.
*   **Adjust to Taste:** The "hot" and "sour" elements are highly personal. Start with the recommended amounts of white pepper and vinegar, then add more incrementally until you achieve your preferred balance. Don't be afraid to experiment!
*   **The Right Thickness:** The cornstarch slurry is key to the soup's characteristic texture. Add it slowly and stir constantly to prevent lumps. If the soup is too thick, add a little more broth; if too thin, mix a bit more slurry and add gradually.
*   **Umami Boost:** Don't discard the rehydrated shiitake mushroom liquid! Strain it and add it to your broth for an extra layer of savory depth.
*   **Freshness Matters:** Fresh ginger and garlic make a significant difference in the soup's aromatic profile.

## Serving Suggestions

Hot and Sour Soup is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. It makes an excellent appetizer for any Chinese meal, pairing beautifully with classics like General Tso's Chicken, Kung Pao Shrimp, or vegetable stir-fries. For a lighter meal, you can serve it with a side of steamed white rice or simple noodles. It's also hearty enough to be a meal on its own, especially if you increase the amount of protein and vegetables. Don't forget the garnish – a sprinkle of fresh cilantro and green onions not only adds color but also a burst of fresh flavor. A drizzle of chili oil at the table can provide an extra kick for those who like it spicier.

## Variations and Substitutions

This versatile Hot and Sour Soup recipe can be easily adapted to suit different dietary preferences and available ingredients:

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Omit the pork and use vegetable broth. Ensure your soy sauce is vegan-friendly. For a vegan version, skip the egg or use a flax egg substitute for binding, though the egg ribbons are a classic element. Increase the amount of tofu and add more mushrooms for heartiness.
*   **Different Proteins:** Instead of pork, you can use shredded chicken, sliced shrimp, or even thinly sliced beef. Add shrimp towards the end of cooking as they cook quickly.
*   **Mushroom Variety:** Experiment with different types of mushrooms like enoki, button, or cremini mushrooms for varied textures and flavors.
*   **Spice Level:** For more heat, add extra white pepper, a few dashes of Tabasco, or a teaspoon of your favorite chili oil during cooking or as a garnish.
*   **Less Sour:** If Chinese black vinegar is too intense, you can use a mix of rice vinegar and a smaller amount of black vinegar, or adjust the quantity to your liking.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Where does Hot and Sour Soup originate?**
A: Hot and Sour Soup is believed to have originated in Sichuan province, China, known for its bold and spicy cuisine. However, variations exist across different regions of China and even in other Asian countries.

**Q: Is Hot and Sour Soup healthy?**
A: Hot and Sour Soup can be a relatively healthy option, packed with vegetables and protein. Its low-fat, broth-based nature makes it a good choice. However, sodium content can be high, so using low-sodium broth and soy sauce is recommended.

**Q: Can I make Hot and Sour Soup ahead of time?**
A: Yes, you can prepare the base of the soup a day in advance. Store it in the refrigerator. When reheating, bring it to a simmer, then add the cornstarch slurry and whisked egg. This helps maintain the best texture.

**Q: What gives Hot and Sour Soup its unique flavor?**
A: The distinctive flavor comes primarily from Chinese black vinegar for sourness and white pepper for heat. These two ingredients, combined with savory broth, soy sauce, and aromatic ginger and garlic, create its complex profile.

**Q: What if I don't have Chinese black vinegar?**
A: While Chinkiang black vinegar is highly recommended for its unique depth, if you can't find it, you can substitute with rice vinegar, adding a tiny splash of balsamic vinegar for a darker hue and deeper flavor. Adjust to taste.

Hot and Sour Soup is more than just a dish; it's an experience – a warming, invigorating, and deeply satisfying journey for your palate. With its perfect balance of hot, sour, savory, and umami flavors, it's no wonder this soup has captured the hearts of food lovers worldwide. Ready to try this incredible Hot and Sour Soup recipe? Gather your ingredients, follow our detailed steps, and prepare to impress yourself and your loved ones with a bowl of authentic Chinese comfort. Enjoy the delightful complexity of this classic, right from your own kitchen!
